title: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures slide with US-Iran peace and inflation in focus
summary: Stock market today: S&P 500, Nasdaq, Dow futures slide with US-Iran peace and inflation in focus
extracted_text: Futures linked to the S&P 500 (ES=F) slipped 0.4%, while contracts tied to the Nasdaq 100 (NQ=F) lost 0.6%. Dow futures (YM=F) declined 0.3%. West Texas Intermediate (CL=F) crude jumped nearly 3% to trade near $78 a barrel, while Brent (BZ=F) crude climbed above $81. President Donald Trump warned Iran of potential additional military action unless Tehran moved to rein in allied groups operating in Lebanon. Attention now turns to Thursday's release of the Personal Consumption Expenditures price index, the Fed's preferred inflation gauge. Economists expect core PCE to show modest acceleration from April levels.

title: Oil prices fall and stock markets rise as US-Iran peace talks progress – business live
summary: Oil prices fall and stock markets rise as US-Iran peace talks progress – business live
extracted_text: Brent crude fell more than 2% to $78.90/barrel. Japan's Nikkei: +1.8%, South Korea's Kospi: +0.6%, MSCI Asia-Pacific ex-Japan index: +0.8%, Chinese blue chips: +1.6%. Iranian foreign ministry spokesperson told Press TV that 'good progress had been made.' Mediators Qatar and Pakistan said both sides agreed to a roadmap with a final deal to be sealed within 60 days. Despite positive tone, US President Trump continued to post threats on social media. Ipek Ozkardeskaya, senior analyst at Swissquote: 'The week starts on a surprisingly positive note despite the uncertainty surrounding the US/Iran peace talks, which took several twists over the weekend.'

title: Crude oil prices climb 2% as Donald Trump issues new threat amid US-Iran peace talks; Brent reaches $82 per barrel
summary: Crude oil prices climb 2% as Donald Trump issues new threat amid US-Iran peace talks; Brent reaches $82 per barrel
extracted_text: Brent crude surged as much as 2.2% at the open, hitting $82.30 per barrel. WTI climbed above $78 per barrel. Trump was quoted as saying by Fox News that he had warned Iranian leaders that any attempt to close the Strait of Hormuz would carry severe consequences, stating that 'they would not make it back to Iran.' Venue: Bürgenstock resort, Switzerland. Talks began Sunday and extended into early Monday, marking start of 60-day negotiation period. Iranian media reported Tehran suspended talks following Trump's warning, but sources said discussions continued. Ponmudi R, CEO of Enrich Money: 'The renewed uncertainty has pushed crude oil prices modestly higher.'

title: World Oil Price per Barrel Today, Monday June 22, 2026
summary: World Oil Price per Barrel Today, Monday June 22, 2026
extracted_text: Brent Crude Oil at $80.22 per barrel, WTI at $77.51 per barrel. Key downward pressures: OPEC+ production easing signals, slowing economies in US and Europe, stronger USD making dollar-denominated oil more expensive, higher-than-expected US crude inventory data. Short-term outlook: oil prices to remain in relatively moderate range. Key factors: international trade negotiations, next OPEC+ production decision, energy demand data from China, US, and India.

title: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
summary: Space Stocks Dip After SpaceX IPO
extracted_text: SpaceX ($SPCX) began trading on NASDAQ on Friday, closing at $161.11 — 19% above IPO target of $135. Space stock dips: Planet Labs -8.8%, Rocket Lab -10.8%, EchoStar -11%, Intuitive Machines -13.1%, AST SpaceMobile -15.5%, Virgin Galactic -31.8%. Micah Walter-Range, Caelus Partners: 'Space-focused investors wanted to capture gains while switching investment to SpaceX.' Upcoming: NASDAQ-100 entry for RKLB expected early July, ETF inclusion, insider lockup expiry after Q2 earnings (20% of stake).

title: S&P 500 Rally Faces Its First Real Test From the Fed
summary: S&P 500 Rally Faces Its First Real Test From the Fed
extracted_text: Marvell Punched Its S&P 500 Ticket for June 22. Marvell was the event-driven chip trade, and the event is a calendar lock. The stock ripped 57% in a month. Question of whether hedge funds have already front-run the inclusion pop.

title: China Shanghai Composite Stock Market Index
summary: China Shanghai Composite Stock Market Index
extracted_text: China's main stock market index, the SHANGHAI, rose to 4131 points on June 22, 2026, gaining 0.99% from the previous session. Over the past month, the index has declined 0.52%, though it remains 22.16% higher than a year ago.

title: Hang Seng Index plunges at noon on Monday, once touching one-year low
summary: Hang Seng Index plunges at noon on Monday, once touching one-year low
extracted_text: Hang Seng Index dipped by nearly 500 points at one point to 23,444, the lowest in one year. It closed 233 points, or 1 percent, at noon. Mixed HK and mainland stocks amid US-Iran talks progress. Hang Seng opened down 113 points or 0.5% at 23,811 before losses accelerated to 389 points or 1.63%.

title: Stock market news for June 18, 2026 - CNBC
extracted_text: The S&P 500 rose 1.08% to end the day at 7,500.58, while the Nasdaq Composite gained 1.91% to settle at 26,517.93. The Dow Jones Industrial Average added 72.15 points, or 0.14%, to 51,564.70. For the week, Nasdaq gained 2.4%, while the Dow industrials and S&P 500 rose nearly 1%.

title: Gold Spot (XAUUSD) Commodity Price — June 22, 2026
extracted_text: Gold rose to 4191.36 USD/t.oz on June 22, 2026, up 0.95% from the previous day. Over the past month, Gold's price has fallen 8.27%, but it is still 24.43% higher than a year ago. Gold reached its all-time high of 5,602.225 USD on Jan 28, 2026.

title: Earnings Calendar and Analysis for This Week (June 22-26)
summary: Earnings Calendar and Analysis for This Week (June 22-26)
extracted_text: There are no noteworthy earnings reports scheduled for release on Monday, June 22. Anticipated releases this week: Micron Technology (MU), Carnival (CCL), FedEx (FDX), Lotus Technology. Commercial Metals (CMC), FactSet (FDS), KB Home (KBH), Blackberry (BB).

title: 10-2 Year Treasury Yield Spread
extracted_text: 10-2 Year Treasury Yield Spread is at 0.27%, compared to 0.29% previous market day and 0.44% last year. 10-year yield: 4.49% (June 19). 2-year yield: 4.18% (June 18).

title: US Dollar Index Hits A New High Since May 2025: What's next?
summary: DXY touched 100.58 on June 22 — highest since May 2025, +1.5% from week's open. Fed dot plot (June 17): 9 of 18 project at least one rate hike in 2026, median year-end fed funds 3.8% (up from 3.4%). New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh declined to submit a dot, stripped forward guidance. US CPI May: +4.2% YoY, core 2.9%. Technical: DXY RSI 70.07 (near overbought), supports at 99.00/99.50, resistance 101.00/101.50.
extracted_text: The US Dollar Index (USDX) touched 100.58 on June 22, 2026 — its highest level since May 2025. The index has gained roughly 1.5% from the week's open. 9 of 18 FOMC participants now project at least one rate hike in 2026. New Fed Chair Kevin Warsh declined to submit a dot and stripped forward guidance from the statement. US CPI (May): +4.2% YoY, core CPI 2.9% YoY. CME FedWatch: December 2026 hike odds fell to ~50% after peace deal (down from ~70% prior week). DXY RSI (14, close): Fast line 70.07 (near overbought). Key pairs: EURUSD support 1.1400/1.1480 resistance 1.1660/1.1900. USDJPY support 158.00/158.50 resistance 160.50/161.00. XAUUSD support 4,150/4,200 resistance 4,350/4,450.

title: Fed interest rate decision June 2026: Fed holds rates steady
summary: FOMC voted unanimously (12-0) to hold rates at 3.50%-3.75%. Statement dramatically shortened from 341 to 130 words, removing dovish bias. Median dot for end-2026: 3.8% (up from 3.4% in March). 9 of 18 participants project at least one rate hike. Headline PCE forecast raised to 3.6% (from 2.7%). Core PCE forecast 3.3% (from 2.7%). GDP growth trimmed to 2.2% (from 2.4%). Unemployment 4.3%. Warsh: 'The commitment to deliver is strong, unanimous, and unambiguous.' Markets priced in a hike as early as October.
extracted_text: FOMC voted unanimously to keep benchmark overnight borrowing rate in 3.5%-3.75% range. Kevin Warsh did not submit a projection. Median end-2026 federal funds rate: 3.8% (up from 3.4% in March) — implying at least one hike this year. Headline PCE inflation 2026 estimate: 3.6% (from 2.7%). Core: 3.3% (from 2.7%). GDP growth: 2.2% (from 2.4%). Unemployment: 4.3%. Recent CPI May: 4.2% headline, 2.9% core. Warsh: 'I did not submit a dot for me. It's not helpful in the conduct of policy. I suspect by year-end there'll be a review about communication broadly.' Markets moved to price in a hike as early as October.

title: Will New US Fed Chair Kevin Warsh Raise Interest Rates This Year?
summary: Warsh's first meeting: rate-hike odds in bond futures surged above 85%. Statement removed dovish bias, committed to 'deliver price stability.' PCE inflation forecast raised to 3.6%. Warsh signaled radical communication changes: forward guidance slashed, dot-plot optional, potential elimination entirely. Alfonzo Bruno (Morningstar): 'It certainly feels like the Fed is starting to lay the groundwork for its next move being a hike.' Rick Rieder (BlackRock): 'While we don't believe a Fed hike is a given, we need to be respectful of that policy potential.'
extracted_text: Rate-hike odds in bond futures markets surged above 85%. Statement removed any dovish bias and explicitly committed to delivering price stability. Median dot for year-end 2026 rose to 3.8% (from 3.4%). PCE inflation forecast sharply raised to 3.6% year-end 2026 (up from 2.7%). Warsh signaled radical changes: forward guidance slashed, dot-plot participation optional, potential elimination of dot plot. Alfonzo Bruno (Morningstar Wealth): 'Based on the removal of the dovish bias, it certainly feels like the Fed is starting to lay the groundwork for its next move being a hike.' Christopher Hodge (Natixis): 'Warsh started his tenure with a bang. He was unambiguously hawkish.' Rick Rieder (BlackRock): 'We need to be respectful of that policy potential.'
